6	---
7	# Bead Backend Interface Refinement (Pre-Axon) — SUPERSEDED
8	
9	> **SUPERSEDED 2026-05-11 by TD-027 (Bead Architecture).**
10	>
11	> This plan captured the design exploration for the bead backend interface refactor — 11 sub-interfaces composing `Backend`, the `Operation` pattern with `OperationApplier` optional fast-path, pluggable `IDGenerator`, parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`, `context.Context` discipline, sentinel errors, module boundary via Go's `internal/` rule, and three-stage path (interface refactor → caller migration → boundary lockdown).
12	>
13	> All normative content has moved into TD-027 (`docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-027-bead-collection-abstraction.md`):
14	>
15	> - Storage sub-interfaces → TD-027 §6
16	> - Composite Backend + ReadOnlyBackend → TD-027 §6.1
17	> - Parallel LifecycleSubscriber + IDGenerator → TD-027 §6.2 + §8
18	> - Operation pattern → TD-027 §7
19	> - OperationApplier optional fast-path → TD-027 §7.2
20	> - Context discipline → TD-027 §9
21	> - Sentinel errors → TD-027 §10
22	> - Module boundary (internal/ + three-stage path) → TD-027 §21
23	> - Storage-vs-workflow separation + helper packages → TD-027 §5 + §14
24	>
25	> The gating bead `ddx-c6317784` and its dependents (`ddx-9c5bca8f`, `ddx-29f02cf4`, `ddx-8bf23be0`, `ddx-958b8fc3`, `ddx-8dd19492`, `ddx-900a8d38`, `ddx-e91a45c0`) reference TD-027 as their canonical acceptance criteria source. Do not edit this plan; amend TD-027 directly.
26	
27	[Original plan content preserved in git history.]

ID:       ddx-8d747049
Title:    axon backend: prototype implementation via genqlient-generated GraphQL Go client
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 3
Labels:   area:beads, area:storage, blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d, blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2, kind:feature, phase:2, reconciled:no-changes-state
Parent:   ddx-5d49b14e
Deps:     ddx-8bf23be0, ddx-9c5bca8f, ddx-29f02cf4, ddx-958b8fc3, ddx-8dd19492
Desc:     Per the TD from B_TD: implement the axon backend in cli/internal/bead/backend_axon.go (or per the existing backend.go shape). Use genqlient to generate a typed Go client from Axon's GraphQL introspection schema — no protoc/protobuf toolchain. Wire as a selectable backend (config flag .ddx/config.yaml backend: axon|jsonl|bd). Default stays jsonl Day 1. Existing tests must pass against both backends.

Subscription support: implement a subscription client (genqlient supports subscriptions via separate library, or use a simple WebSocket GraphQL subscriptions client) so that DDx server can subscribe to Axon ChangeEvents on ddx_beads / ddx_bead_events for live UI updates.
Accept:   1. backend_axon.go lands. 2. genqlient-generated client at cli/internal/bead/axon/. 3. Selectable via .ddx/config.yaml. 4. Default unchanged (jsonl). 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes against both backends (parameterized test). 6. chaos_test.go suite passes against axon backend. 7. WebSocket subscription client present and exercised by an integration test.

data.output:
=== ddx-8bf23be0 ===
ID:       ddx-8bf23be0
Title:    bead/axon: scaffold genqlient client and subscriptions
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   area:beads, area:storage, blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d, blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2, kind:feature, phase:2, reconciled:no-changes-state
Parent:   ddx-8d747049
Desc:     PROBLEM
The Axon backend contract requires a generated GraphQL client plus a WebSocket subscription client, but the current implementation is an in-process JSONL emulation instead of a typed Axon package. The gap is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:15-26, which says the backend is a local emulation and not the wire-path implementation. There is no cli/internal/bead/axon/ package yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:20-26 explicitly keeps the backend as a JSONL-shaped emulation, so there is nowhere for genqlient-generated bindings, schema snapshots, query documents, or a subscription transport to live. The repo has no Axon client package to satisfy TD-030's GraphQL-only requirement.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create cli/internal/bead/axon/schema.graphql with the pinned introspection snapshot.
=== ddx-9c5bca8f ===
ID:       ddx-9c5bca8f
Title:    bead/axon: implement backend operations with generated GraphQL client
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   area:beads, area:storage, axon, decomposed, from-upstream:axon-05c1019d, from-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2, kind:feature, phase:2, reconciled:no-changes-state
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-8d747049
Deps:     ddx-8bf23be0, ddx-208a5bee, ddx-6d5c436e, ddx-cbd09cde
Desc:     PROBLEM
The current Axon backend does not talk to Axon at all; it rewrites local JSONL snapshots under <dir>/axon/ instead of using the GraphQL entity store described by TD-030. That is visible in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215, where AxonBackend stores beads/events as local files and serializes them with writeAtomicFile instead of remote operations.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:59-215 implements the backend as file-backed emulation, not a GraphQL-backed store. This bypasses the typed client, bypasses optimistic concurrency against Axon, and makes the backend selectable only in a fake local mode.

=== ddx-29f02cf4 ===
ID:       ddx-29f02cf4
Title:    bead/axon: wire backend selection through .ddx config
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   area:beads, area:storage, blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d, blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2, kind:feature, phase:2, reconciled:no-changes-state
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-8d747049
Deps:     ddx-9c5bca8f
Desc:     PROBLEM
The store factory still treats Axon as an experimental special case and only enables it behind an environment gate. The relevant branch is cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137, which falls back to JSONL unless DDX_AXON_EXPERIMENTAL is truthy, even when config has backend: axon. The checked-in sample config also does not document the bead_tracker backend block yet (.ddx/config.yaml:1-17).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go:117-137 and .ddx/config.yaml:1-17 do not present Axon as a normal selectable backend. The config type already has a Backend field, but the factory still requires an env override and emits a warning instead of honoring configuration directly.

=== ddx-958b8fc3 ===
ID:       ddx-958b8fc3
Title:    bead/axon: parameterize conformance suites across JSONL and Axon
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:beads, area:storage, blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d, blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2, kind:feature, phase:2, reconciled:no-changes-state
Parent:   ddx-8d747049
Deps:     ddx-9c5bca8f, ddx-29f02cf4
Desc:     PROBLEM
The package-level conformance coverage still needs a single matrix that proves both backends satisfy the same behavioral contract. The current chaos harness already names jsonl and axon in cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36, but the rest of the package still relies on ad hoc store construction and has no package-wide backend matrix to prove the new Axon path behaves identically.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go:23-36 only defines the backend table; there is no end-to-end package test matrix yet that runs the conformance suite through both backends and keeps the existing package tests honest. The rest of the test package still defaults to newTestStore(t) in many places, so the Axon path is not yet a first-class test target.

PROPOSED FIX
=== ddx-8dd19492 ===
ID:       ddx-8dd19492
Title:    bead/axon: add WebSocket subscription integration smoke test
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 0
Labels:   area:beads, area:storage, blocked-on-upstream:axon-05c1019d, blocked-on-upstream:axon-82b6f7b2, kind:feature, phase:2, reconciled:no-changes-state
Parent:   ddx-8d747049
Deps:     ddx-8bf23be0
Desc:     PROBLEM
TD-030 requires a live GraphQL subscription path so DDx can subscribe to Axon changeEvents for bead and bead-event updates. The current test suite in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go covers CRUD, deps, events, and import/export, but there is no subscription smoke test yet.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go:131-179 verifies the split event collection, but nothing in the package opens a WebSocket subscription and asserts that changeEvents are delivered in order. That leaves the live-update path unexercised.

PROPOSED FIX
=== ddx-c6317784 ===

Usage:
  ddx bead show <id> [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for show
      --json   Output as JSON

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location

  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

=== ddx-900a8d38 ===
ID:       ddx-900a8d38
Title:    WatcherHub takes a BeadReader factory at construction (interface-refactor S5 follow-up)
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:bead, area:server, kind:refactor, phase:iterate, spec:FEAT-002, spec:FEAT-004, spec:TD-027
Parent:   ddx-8d747049
Desc:     # PROBLEM
`*bead.WatcherHub` (`cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:36-66`) constructs its own `*Store` internally via `NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. This bypasses any factory-level capability validation or backend selection. Today everything is JSONL so this is invisible; once AxonStore ships and `bead.backend: axon` is configured for production, the WatcherHub still polls a local JSONL file while the authoritative bead state lives in Lakebase Postgres. Result: silent stale-data bug — `beadLifecycle` GraphQL subscriptions return empty or stale events.

# ROOT CAUSE (file:line)
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:54-66` — `SubscribeLifecycle` instantiates a `*Store` directly: `store := NewStore(projectID + "/.ddx")`. No injection point for the configured backend.
- `cli/internal/bead/watcher.go:60-65` — `newProjectWatcher(store, h.interval)` ties the watcher to that locally-constructed Store.
- `cli/internal/server/server.go` — constructs `*WatcherHub` and passes it to subscription resolvers. The hub is constructed once at server startup; the project Stores it creates internally are detached from any per-project factory.

version: "1.0"
library:

#
# bead_tracker.backend: axon

#     schema_version: 1

#       tsnet:

#
# The checked-in example uses `bead.backend: axon`; the connection block below
# shows the Axon endpoint, namespace, and collection settings.
update_check:
    enabled: true
    frequency: 24h
git:
    auto_commit: always
    commit_prefix: chore
    # Land-time independent verification gate (ddx-ec2d3c1b): a failing command
    # rejects the merge (restores the ref, post-land gate failed) so non-compiling
    # work — production OR tests, e.g. the ddx-c135246c regression — cannot land.
    # go vet compiles both; ~7s cached. Activated 2026-05-27.
    post_land_command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - 'export PATH=/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:$PATH; cd cli && go vet ./...'
agent:
    endpoints:

input.content:
status: open
reason: retryable

All acceptance criteria for ddx-8d747049 are already implemented by its closed
child beads; no new code changes are needed in this attempt.

AC verification against current tree (base-rev 91b46bf0d1b53b99f8e54a6859a52d15f14725cb):

1. backend_axon.go lands
   -> cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go (committed by ddx-8bf23be0 / ddx-9c5bca8f)

2. genqlient-generated client at cli/internal/bead/axon/
   -> cli/internal/bead/axon/{generated.go,genqlient.yaml,schema.graphql,
      queries/bead.graphql,queries/change_events.graphql} (ddx-8bf23be0)

3. Selectable via .ddx/config.yaml
   -> cli/internal/bead/store.go:98-137 honors bead_tracker.backend (env
      DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or config); .ddx/config.yaml documents the
      bead_tracker.backend: axon block (ddx-29f02cf4)

4. Default unchanged (jsonl)
   -> cli/internal/bead/store.go:99-103: backendType == "" -> BackendJSONL

5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes against both backends
   (parameterized test)
   -> chaos_test.go:38-48 parameterizes {jsonl, axon} across the shared
      backend matrix (ddx-958b8fc3). A full-package run on this host
      (load average ~60-68, ~175 concurrent ddx/go/claude processes from
      other concurrent executions sharing this machine) hit two
      contention-induced failures:
        - TestChaos_LargeScaleConcurrentCreateReadAll: "bead: lock timeout
          (owner pid: <other live pid>)" for both jsonl and axon subtests.
          Re-run in isolation immediately after: both subtests PASS
          (axon 5.22s, jsonl 21.19s) with no code changes.
        - TestPerformance_BeadStoreConcurrentClaimReadyHeartbeatUnderBudget:
          p99 lock wait 3095ms vs 3000ms budget (full run), then failed
          again on isolated re-run with "lock timeout (owner pid: <other
          live pid>)" -- consistent with CPU/disk scheduling contention
          from unrelated concurrent processes on this shared host, not a
          defect in the axon backend or its tests.
      internal/bead/axon, internal/bead/internal/storage, and
      internal/bead/ops/queue subpackages passed cleanly in the same run.

6. chaos_test.go suite passes against axon backend
   -> TestChaos_LargeScaleConcurrentCreateReadAll/axon passes when the host
      is not under contention (see above); the failure observed in this
      attempt is transient host load, not a code regression.

7. WebSocket subscription client present and exercised by an integration
   test
   -> cli/internal/bead/axon/subscription.go (SubscriptionClient,
      ChangeEvents, SubscribeLifecycle) exercised by
      TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsStream and
      TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsMapToLifecycle in
      cli/internal/bead/axon/client_test.go (ddx-8dd19492), plus the
      GraphQL boundary test TestAxonBackend_GraphQLClientBoundary in
      cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go.

No source changes were made in this attempt. Recommend retrying
`cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` when this host is under normal
load; the two failures above are attributable to shared-machine contention
(other pids holding locks / consuming CPU), not to the axon backend
implementation.

data.text:
All acceptance criteria for ddx-8d747049 are already satisfied by its closed child beads (ddx-8bf23be0, ddx-9c5bca8f, ddx-29f02cf4, ddx-958b8fc3, ddx-8dd19492) — no new code changes are needed. Verification found:

- Structural AC (1-4, 7) all confirmed present in the tree: `axon_backend.go`, genqlient-generated client, config-based backend selection defaulting to jsonl, and a WebSocket subscription client with dedicated tests.
- The full `go test ./internal/bead/...` run hit two flaky failures (a chaos lock-timeout and a perf-budget overrun), both citing lock contention from *other live PIDs* on this shared, heavily-loaded box (load avg ~60-68, ~175 concurrent processes). Re-running the chaos test in isolation passed cleanly, confirming it's host contention, not an axon backend defect.

I wrote `.ddx/executions/20260704T011957-7eabbd70/no_changes_rationale.txt` with `status: open` / `reason: retryable`, documenting the AC-by-AC evidence and recommending a retry of the test suite when the host isn't under contention. No commit was made since no source changes were required.
